title: Roadmap: jjforge open epics in priority order
body:
This ticket is the running roadmap. Open epics appear in the order
they should be tackled. As work shifts, the order shifts, and the
body is edited in place (`jjf update 9566f52 --body-file new.md`).

**This ticket never closes.**

# Current priority order (as of 2026-06-22, post git-bug cutover)

Above the line: shipping next. Below the line: queued behind the
shipping work.

1. **`epic:agent-ergonomics`** (`5a755ec`) — `ready`, `remember`,
   atomic claim, MCP server. The CLI verb set, the JSON contract, AND
   the sync loop are now all in place — this is the natural next epic.

2. **`epic:mvp-cli`** (`a5f8122`) — effectively done.
   `cli-replace-shim` remains; **unblocked** as of the cutover
   (migration decision = B = start fresh, recorded in archived
   git-bug `d12031c`). Flipping `bin/jjf` from a `git-bug` shell
   shim into a thin Rust-binary wrapper (or dropping it entirely)
   is the last piece of `epic:mvp-cli`.

3. **`epic:mvp-sync`** (`54caedd`) — feature-complete. Will only
   re-surface if a real consumer uncovers a gap.

4. **`epic:mvp-storage`** (`f21b950`) — feature-complete.

---

5. **`epic:multi-client`** (`99f01ed`) — sketched. Don't pull above
   the line until the MVP path closes.

6. **`epic:project-agent-orchestration`** (`585b84b`) — sketched,
   vision-level.

# Reader's contract

- The body above IS the truth. When priorities shift, edit the body
  via `jjf update 9566f52 --body-file <path>` and (optionally) drop
  a `jjf comment 9566f52` announcing the change.
- For "what exists" — every issue, by label, by status — use the
  jjforge queries in `CLAUDE.md`, not a maintained index.
- Don't expand scope into epics below the line until this roadmap
  explicitly pulls them up.

# History

This ticket was re-filed in jjforge on 2026-06-22 during the git-bug
cutover (decision in archived git-bug ticket `d12031c` — see
`git-bug bug show d12031c` against `refs/bugs/*`). The pre-cutover
ticket lived at archived `6a65c0d`; its comment history (seven status
updates 2026-06-21 through 2026-06-22 carrying the priority changes
and the migration design call) is preserved in git refs and read via
`git-bug bug show 6a65c0d`.

The cutover doc at `docs/git-bug-cutover.md` is the bridge.
